name: lmstudio-code
description: Agentic coding panel for your local LM Studio models. A Claude Code / Codex–style chat experience powered by the open-source OpenCode agent, running entirely against your local models.

commands.title: LM Studio Code: Open Chat in Editor Tab
LM Studio Code: Restart OpenCode Server

lmstudioCode.lmStudioBaseUrl.description: Base URL of the LM Studio OpenAI-compatible server (must end in /v1).

lmstudioCode.opencodePath.description: Override path to an opencode binary. Leave empty to use your own install (PATH, ~/.opencode/bin/opencode) if present, otherwise the binary bundled with this extension.

lmstudioCode.serverPort.description: Port for the embedded OpenCode server. 0 = pick a free port automatically.

lmstudioCode.defaultModel.description: Model id to select by default (must match an LM Studio model id). Empty = first available.

lmstudioCode.agent.description: OpenCode agent. 'build' can edit files and run tools; 'plan' is read-only.

lmstudioCode.autoEnsureContext.description: Before prompting, ensure the selected LM Studio model is loaded with an adequate context length (uses the lms CLI). Prevents the 'tokens to keep > context length' error from small default context windows.

lmstudioCode.minContextLength.description: Minimum context length to (re)load models with when autoEnsureContext is enabled. OpenCode's agent system prompt + tools alone use ~11k tokens, so keep this comfortably large.

lmstudioCode.gpuOffload.description: GPU offload ratio passed to 'lms load' when reloading a model for context (off | max | 0..1).

type.description: Transport. Local/stdio runs a command; remote/http/sse connects to a URL. Inferred from the fields when omitted.

command.description: Executable to run for a local server (string), with arguments in 'args'.

args.description: Arguments passed to the local server command.

env.description: Environment variables for a local server. Values may use ${VAR} references.

url.description: Endpoint URL for a remote server.

headers.description: HTTP headers for a remote server. Values may use ${VAR} references (e.g. an API token).

enabled.description: Set false to keep the server defined but not started.

scripts.test: npm run check-types:test && node esbuild.test.js && node --test "out-test/**/*.test.js"
scripts.test:integration: npm run compile && npm run compile:integration && node out-integration/test-integration/runTests.js
